source_reviews.notes: The official page confirms that BERD covers for-profit businesses performing or funding R&D and supports the disclosed scope mismatch. It does not measure owner age or succession shortage, so those score inputs remain explicitly labeled estimates without direct support.

The article supports bounded AI use cases, partnerships, and gradual rather than overnight change in drug discovery. It does not measure effective adoption or time-to-50% among small preclinical CROs, as disclosed.

The FDA page supports the claim of more than 500 drug and biological product submissions with AI components since 2016 and the credibility/context-of-use framework. It is not an adoption-prevalence measure for small CROs, as disclosed.

The page supports preclinical-CRO service context and the existence and mechanics of T&M/FTE, fixed-price, milestone, bundled, retainer, and outcome-linked pricing. It does not establish the contract mix or capture rate of small US targets, as disclosed.

The release confirms a public CRO with discovery, safety-assessment, translational-science, and research-model operations. It does not directly quantify automatable task share, moat, or the count of active consolidators for the target segment.

The page confirms 78 biopharma-services deals, 56 financial-sponsor transactions, and selected broad-sector EBITDA multiples including Fortrea's 11.5x divestiture. It does not provide size-matched preclinical-CRO entry/exit comps or a seven-buyer census, as disclosed.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 5-digit role mix is a proxy and its 62.15% residual combines physical lab, quality and administrative work.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions are task-replaceability judgments, not observed headcount reductions or adoption forecasts.
inputs.pi_dist: No observed contract-type mix for US $1-10M EBITDA NAICS 541714 providers was reopened.
inputs.pi_dist: Global preclinical-CRO pricing categories are a disclosed proxy for the target segment.
inputs.pi_moat: Public operator and market evidence do not measure target-specific customer concentration, assay differentiation or sponsor switching costs.
inputs.t50_years: BCG is a 2022 strategic publication and FDA submission experience is not a prevalence survey of small CROs.
inputs.t50_years: Effective adoption is stricter than use of an AI tool or an AI-supported submission.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No direct NAICS 541714 adoption census was found; this is not an observed firm-share statistic.
inputs.historical_analogs: This qualitative analogue is not a measured adoption curve for contract research laboratories.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No direct owner-age statistic for EBITDA-positive NAICS 541714 targets was reopened.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of reopened documentation is not evidence that no succession issue exists.
inputs.active_consolidators: This is not a census of NAICS 541714 buyers and broader biopharma-services deal activity includes clinical, CDMO and other adjacent businesses.
inputs.buy_mult: KPMG's disclosed examples are broader biopharma services and are not size-matched preclinical-CRO buy comps.
inputs.buy_mult: No direct $1-10M EBITDA NAICS 541714 transaction series was reopened.
inputs.exit_mult: The cited 11.5x example is not a preclinical-CRO, small-platform or NAICS 541714 exit comparable.
inputs.exit_mult: No direct small-lab exit series was reopened.
inputs.pricing_structure: The source describes the global preclinical-CRO market rather than the actual contract mix of small US targets.
summary: All deterministic mechanics pass and every registered source was accessible. The cautious kill thesis remains useful: sources support the direction of gradual AI adoption, durable physical and regulated work, mixed pricing mechanics, and broad biopharma-services deal activity. The score is nevertheless built from disclosed, low-confidence proxies for small-CRO adoption, owner age, buyer count, contract mix, and entry/exit multiples; these are publication caveats rather than material defects because the proxy limitations are explicit and the conclusion is conservative.
